title: Finish a central package security update in locked mode
prompt: A security update to the centrally managed `Moon.Security.Json` package was started but not finished. `Directory.Packages.props` and the repository vulnerability policy already select the approved version, while committed lock snapshots still describe the blocked prior release. As a result, locked restore fails in both projects. Finish the update by bringing every lock file into exact agreement with central package management. Preserve locked, offline restore and the NuGet audit policy. The Desktop project intentionally references `Moon.Windows.CredentialStore` only for its Windows target; retain that conditional reference and the corresponding target-specific lock entry. Do not add project-local versions, weaken audit or locked-mode settings, change package sources, modify application code, or edit tests. The only intended production changes are the stale lock snapshots. Verification is offline and deterministic with `python3 -B tests/verify.py`.

full_distill.primary_skill: synchronize centrally managed NuGet versions with committed per-project lock files while retaining intentional target-conditioned dependencies and locked security policy
full_distill.baseline_evidence: the independent audit finds Moon.Security.Json 4.2.0 in both lock files even though central management and policy require 4.2.1, and dotnet restore --locked-mode rejects the stale snapshots
full_distill.success_contract: all direct lock entries match the central pins and local package hashes, the blocked version disappears, the Windows-only package remains confined to net10.0-windows, NuGet security and locked-mode controls remain enabled, and isolated offline restore plus compilation succeed
adversarial_factors: the verifier derives direct dependency expectations from project conditions and central pins instead of accepting a hard-coded replacement lock shape
local nupkg SHA-512 hashes are recomputed and compared with every lock entry, rejecting invented or copied content hashes
the dependency policy independently rejects the superseded security version and audits NuGet audit mode, severity, and warnings-as-errors settings
the multi-target Desktop lock must retain Moon.Windows.CredentialStore only under net10.0-windows, so flattening or deleting the conditional dependency fails
restore and build run in an isolated copy with cleared remote sources, locked mode, an empty package cache, and telemetry and first-run behavior disabled
